methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $55M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Stark Defence

data.narrative:
Blackshark.ai is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Michael Putz that has raised $55M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the same sector and stage, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $55M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression, implying a $110M valuation. The peer-median approach examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations of $500M (Stark Defence), $300M (Vatn Systems), and $150M (Ares Industries), establishing a median of $300M and a range of $150M–$500M.

Combining both methods yields a triangulated valuation range of $94M to $345M, with a median of $205M. The wide dispersion between the stage-progression result ($110M) and peer median ($300M) reflects material uncertainty around whether Blackshark.ai's capital efficiency and market position align with higher-valued peers or the lower bound of the comp set. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, customer concentration, and technical differentiation relative to the sampled peer group.
